name: Metrics Model: Starter Project Health
description: An introduction to drawing community performance conclusions from four fundamental open-source health metrics.
author: CHAOSS Project
abstract: This resource provides a lightweight starting point for measuring the overall viability of a repository without overwhelming data analysis. It concentrates on tracking Time to First Response, Change Request Closure Ratio, Release Frequency, and Contributor Absence Factor.
audience: Open source maintainers, community evaluators, software engineers checking dependencies
learningObjectives:
After this lesson, the learner should be able to:
- Identify the four foundational metrics in the CHAOSS Starter Project Health model
- Explain what Time to First Response, Change Request Closure Ratio, Release Frequency, and Contributor Absence Factor measure
- Apply these metrics to evaluate repository responsiveness and contributor risk
- Use this model as an entry point for deeper CHAOSS metrics work
teaches: Evaluate an open source repository using four foundational quantitative metrics to identify community friction areas.
competencyRequired: Basic understanding of repository processes such as pull requests, issues, and tagged releases
